tasks.name: Project Scaffolding & pyproject.toml
tasks.description: Initialize the Python package structure. Create the full directory tree as defined in the context document Section 6.1. Write pyproject.toml with all required dependencies pinned to specific versions: Python>=3.11, typer==0.12.*, langgraph==0.2.*, pandas==2.*, polars==0.20.*, openpyxl==3.*, chardet==5.*, pyyaml==6.*, pydantic==2.*, httpx==0.27.*, rich==13.*, ruff (dev). Define [project.scripts] entry: ave = 'ave.cli:app'. Create ave/__init__.py with __version__ = '0.1.0'. Create empty __init__.py in all subpackages: ave/pipeline/, ave/models/, ave/engines/, ave/storage/, ave/export/, ave/utils/, tests/unit/, tests/integration/, tests/fixtures/. Create .gitignore, ruff.toml with line-length=100.
acceptance_criteria: pip install -e . completes without errors
python -c 'import ave' succeeds
All subdirectories exist as defined in Section 6.1
ruff check . returns 0 errors on empty package
edge_cases: Ensure ave/pipeline/ does not accidentally become a namespace package (has __init__.py)
pyproject.toml must include optional dependency group for pdf: weasyprint>=62

tasks.name: Custom Exception Hierarchy
tasks.description: Create ave/exceptions.py with the full exception hierarchy from Section 10.1. Implement: AveError(Exception) as base with message and optional cause; ConfigError(AveError); IngestionError(AveError); RuleValidationError(AveError) with optional rule_id field; PipelineError(AveError) with optional layer field (int); LLMError(AveError); LLMUnavailableError(LLMError) with provider field; LLMResponseError(LLMError) with raw_response field; StorageError(AveError). Each exception must accept a message: str and optional cause: Exception. Implement __str__ to include cause chain if present.
acceptance_criteria: All 9 exception classes defined and importable
AveError is base for all others (isinstance checks pass)
LLMUnavailableError and LLMResponseError are subclasses of LLMError
Each exception str() includes the message and cause if provided
raise IngestionError('msg') from original_exc preserves __cause__
edge_cases: PipelineError.layer must accept None for pre-pipeline errors
RuleValidationError.rule_id must accept None for YAML-level parse errors

tasks.name: Hashing Utilities Module
tasks.description: Create ave/utils/hashing.py. Implement: hash_file(path: Path) -> str — reads file in 64KB chunks, returns lowercase hex SHA-256 digest; hash_string(s: str) -> str — encodes as UTF-8, returns SHA-256 hex digest; hash_dict(d: dict) -> str — serializes dict with sorted keys via json.dumps(sort_keys=True), encodes UTF-8, returns SHA-256 hex; hash_dataframe(df: Any) -> str — converts DataFrame to canonical CSV string (sorted columns, sorted rows), hashes the string; verify_hash(data: Any, expected_hash: str) -> bool — accepts file Path, str, or dict, dispatches to correct hash function, returns comparison result. All hashes must be lowercase 64-character hex strings.
acceptance_criteria: hash_file returns identical hash for same file content regardless of filename
hash_dict is deterministic: same keys/values in different insertion order produce same hash
hash_dataframe is deterministic for same data regardless of row order
All hashes are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters
verify_hash returns False for any tampered input
edge_cases: hash_file must handle files > 1GB without loading into memory (use chunked reading)
hash_dict must handle nested dicts and lists (recursive serialization via json.dumps)
hash_dataframe: sort both columns and rows before hashing to ensure order-independence

tasks.name: Structured Logging Setup
tasks.description: Create ave/utils/logging.py. Implement: setup_logging(verbose: bool = False, debug: bool = False, log_file: Optional[Path] = None) -> logging.Logger — configures root 'ave' logger; get_logger(name: str) -> logging.Logger — returns child logger. Log format must be: '2025-05-01T14:23:01+07:00 | {level:<8} | {name:<20} | {message}'. Use Python standard logging with rich.logging.RichHandler for console output (with markup=False to avoid formatting interference). If log_file provided, also add FileHandler with same format. Verbose=True sets level to INFO; debug=True sets level to DEBUG; default is WARNING. Logger name passed to get_logger should be the module name (e.g., 'layer1_ingestion').
acceptance_criteria: Log format matches exactly: timestamp | level | module | message
Timestamp includes timezone offset (not UTC Z suffix)
Verbose mode shows INFO and above
Debug mode shows all levels including DEBUG
File handler writes plain text (no ANSI codes)
Console handler uses rich formatting
edge_cases: Log file directory must be created if it does not exist
Logger must not propagate to root logger to avoid duplicate output
Calling setup_logging twice must not add duplicate handlers

tasks.name: Finding & TrailEntry Dataclasses
tasks.description: Create ave/models/finding.py and ave/models/trail.py. In finding.py: implement Finding dataclass exactly matching Section 4.3 schema. All fields as specified. Add to_dict() -> dict method that serializes all fields including row_data (JSON-safe). Add from_dict(d: dict) -> 'Finding' classmethod. In trail.py: implement TrailEntry dataclass matching Section 2.6.1 schema. Fields: session_id (str), entry_id (str, default=uuid4), timestamp (str, default=utcnow ISO8601 with timezone), agent_id (str), action_type (str), input_hash (str), output_hash (str), reasoning_summary (str, max 500 chars enforced via __post_init__), human_decision (Optional[str]), human_decision_timestamp (Optional[str]), human_decision_note (Optional[str]), confidence (float, 0.0-1.0), duration_ms (int), llm_model_used (Optional[str]), llm_tokens_used (Optional[int]). Add to_jsonl_line() -> str that returns JSON string without newline. Add from_jsonl_line(line: str) -> 'TrailEntry' classmethod.
acceptance_criteria: Finding.to_dict() produces JSON-serializable dict (json.dumps succeeds)
Finding.from_dict(finding.to_dict()) round-trips losslessly
TrailEntry.reasoning_summary raises ValueError if > 500 chars in __post_init__
TrailEntry.confidence raises ValueError if outside [0.0, 1.0] in __post_init__
TrailEntry.to_jsonl_line() produces valid single-line JSON (no embedded newlines)
TrailEntry timestamps default to current UTC time with timezone suffix
edge_cases: Finding.row_data dict may contain datetime objects — serialize to ISO string in to_dict()
Finding.actual_value and expected_value may be numeric, string, None, or list — serialize all as strings
TrailEntry.entry_id must be a valid UUID v4 string

tasks.name: Source Manifest Model
tasks.description: Create ave/models/manifest.py. Implement ColumnMeta dataclass: original_name (str), normalized_name (str), inferred_dtype (str: 'date'|'numeric'|'currency'|'text'|'boolean'|'mixed'), null_count (int), null_ratio (float), unique_count (int), sample_values (list[str], max 3 values). Implement SourceManifest dataclass matching Section 4.2 schema exactly. Add to_dict() -> dict method. Add from_dict() classmethod. The source_file sub-object must include: path, filename, format, size_bytes, sha256_hash, encoding, sheet_name. The schema sub-object must include: row_count_raw, row_count_after_cleaning, column_count, dropped_empty_rows, dropped_empty_cols, columns (list of ColumnMeta), parse_warnings (list of str).
acceptance_criteria: SourceManifest.to_dict() is JSON-serializable
All required fields from Section 4.2 are present
sample_values list is capped at 3 items
null_ratio is float between 0.0 and 1.0
edge_cases: sheet_name is None for CSV files — serialize as null in JSON
sample_values must be strings even if original values are numeric

tasks.name: AveConfig Pydantic Model & YAML Loader
tasks.description: Create ave/config.py. Implement AveConfig Pydantic BaseModel covering all fields from Section 4.4 config YAML schema. Sub-models: PipelineConfig, IngestionConfig, ColumnConfig, IntegrityConfig, LLMConfig, OutputConfig. Implement load_config(path: Path) -> AveConfig — reads YAML file, validates against schema, raises ConfigError with field-level error messages on validation failure. Implement get_default_config() -> AveConfig — returns config with all defaults from Section 4.4. Validators: max_rows must be 1-50000; llm.provider must be 'ollama'|'groq'|'mistral'; output.formats must be subset of ['json','markdown','pdf']; llm.temperature must be 0.0-1.0; llm.batch_size must be 1-500. The rules_file field should resolve relative paths relative to the config file's directory.
acceptance_criteria: load_config('minimal_config.yaml') succeeds with only required fields present
load_config with invalid provider raises ConfigError with field name
All defaults match values specified in Section 4.4
rules_file relative path is resolved to absolute path during loading
Pydantic validation errors are caught and re-raised as ConfigError
edge_cases: YAML file with tabs instead of spaces raises ConfigError with line number
Missing YAML file raises ConfigError with file path in message
llm.enabled: false means LLM fields are optional and not validated for connectivity

tasks.name: PipelineContext Dataclass
tasks.description: Create ave/context.py. Implement PipelineContext dataclass exactly matching Section 5.1.1 schema. Fields as specified. Add helper methods: add_error(message: str) -> None — appends to errors list and logs at ERROR level; add_warning(message: str) -> None — appends to warnings list and logs at WARNING level; get_all_findings() -> List[Finding] — returns integrity_findings + anomaly_findings + verified_findings deduplicated by finding_id; has_fatal_error() -> bool — returns True if errors list is non-empty; to_checkpoint_dict() -> dict — serializes serializable fields (excludes DataFrames, trail_writer, llm_client) for checkpoint storage.
acceptance_criteria: get_all_findings() deduplicates by finding_id
to_checkpoint_dict() is JSON-serializable (no DataFrame objects)
has_fatal_error() returns True iff errors list is non-empty
add_error logs at ERROR level via ave logger
edge_cases: get_all_findings() must handle case where any findings list is None (not yet set)
to_checkpoint_dict() must serialize Finding objects via their to_dict() method

tasks.name: SQLite Database Module
tasks.description: Create ave/storage/database.py. Implement AveDatabase class with __init__(db_path: Path) that creates the database and all 4 tables on first connection (using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS). Implement all CRUD methods: create_session(session: dict) -> None; update_session(session_id: str, updates: dict) -> None; get_session(session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]; list_sessions(limit: int = 20) -> List[dict]; save_finding(finding: Finding) -> None; get_findings(session_id: str) -> List[Finding]; save_rule_snapshot(session_id: str, rules: List[dict]) -> None; save_llm_call(call_data: dict) -> None; get_llm_stats(session_id: str) -> dict. Use context manager for connections (with sqlite3.connect() as conn). Implement retry logic: if OperationalError ('database is locked'), retry 3 times with 100ms sleep, then raise StorageError. All timestamps stored as ISO 8601 strings. Findings serialized via Finding.to_dict().
acceptance_criteria: All 4 tables created on first instantiation
save_finding stores all fields correctly and get_findings round-trips them
SQLite locked error triggers retry 3 times before raising StorageError
list_sessions returns sessions ordered by created_at DESC
update_session raises StorageError if session_id not found
edge_cases: db_path directory must be created if it doesn't exist
Concurrent write attempt (two processes) must retry not crash
Finding.row_data is stored as JSON text and deserialized on read

tasks.name: TrailWriter Engine
tasks.description: Create ave/engines/trail_writer.py. Implement TrailWriter class. __init__(session_id: str, output_dir: Path) — sets trail file path to output_dir/{session_id}_trail.jsonl, creates file with mode 'a' and sets permissions to 0o600. write(entry: TrailEntry) -> None — appends entry.to_jsonl_line() + newline to JSONL file; also logs at DEBUG level. finalize() -> str — computes SHA-256 hash of entire file content up to this point, writes a special CHAIN_HASH entry with the hash, closes file, returns file path string. verify(trail_path: Path) -> bool — reads all entries from trail file, finds the last CHAIN_HASH entry, recomputes hash of all preceding content, returns True if hashes match. The CHAIN_HASH entry format: {'action_type': 'CHAIN_HASH', 'chain_hash': '<hex>', ...other required fields}.
acceptance_criteria: JSONL file is append-only — no existing lines are ever modified
File permissions are 0o600 on creation
verify() returns True for an unmodified trail file
verify() returns False if any character in any non-CHAIN_HASH line is changed
finalize() can only be called once per session (raise TrailError if called twice)
Each write() call produces exactly one line in the JSONL file
edge_cases: verify() on a trail file with no CHAIN_HASH entry returns False
If output_dir does not exist, create it in __init__
write() after finalize() must raise an error (trail is closed)

tasks.name: Checkpoint Save/Load Module
tasks.description: Create ave/storage/checkpoint.py. Implement CheckpointManager class. __init__(session_id: str, output_dir: Path) — sets checkpoint dir to output_dir/.checkpoints/{session_id}/. save_checkpoint(layer: int, context: PipelineContext) -> None — serializes context.to_checkpoint_dict() to {checkpoint_dir}/layer_{layer}.json; also writes a manifest.json listing saved layers. load_checkpoint(session_id: str, output_dir: Path) -> Optional[dict] — returns the checkpoint dict for the highest completed layer, or None if no checkpoint exists. has_checkpoint(session_id: str, output_dir: Path) -> bool — returns True if checkpoint directory has at least one layer file. clear_checkpoint(session_id: str, output_dir: Path) -> None — deletes entire checkpoint directory for session.
acceptance_criteria: save_checkpoint for layer 3 creates layer_3.json in checkpoint dir
load_checkpoint returns dict from highest-numbered layer file
has_checkpoint returns False if .checkpoints/{session_id}/ doesn't exist
clear_checkpoint removes entire checkpoint directory
Checkpoint JSON is valid and loadable after power-loss simulation (flush to disk)
edge_cases: Checkpoint directory permissions: set to 0o700
save_checkpoint must use atomic write (write to temp file then rename) to prevent corruption
If layer_3.json exists but layer_2.json does not, load layer_3.json (don't require sequential)

tasks.name: Value Normalization Utilities
tasks.description: Create ave/utils/normalization.py. Implement the following functions: detect_locale(series: Any) -> str — analyzes a numeric string series, detects 'vi' (Vietnamese: . as thousands, , as decimal) or 'en' (English: , as thousands, . as decimal) by examining patterns in at least 20 sample values. Returns 'vi' or 'en'. normalize_number(value: str, locale: str = 'auto') -> Optional[float] — strips currency symbols, thousands separators, converts decimal separator, returns float or None if unparseable. normalize_date(value: str) -> Optional[str] — tries all 5 date formats from Section 2.1.3, returns ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) or None if all fail. normalize_currency(value: str) -> Tuple[Optional[float], Optional[str]] — extracts numeric amount and currency code. strip_pii_if_needed(df, columns_to_strip: List[str]) -> DataFrame — returns DataFrame copy with specified columns replaced by '[REDACTED]'. normalize_text(value: str) -> str — strips whitespace, normalizes Unicode to NFC. to_snake_case(name: str) -> str — converts column name to snake_case (handle spaces, hyphens, Vietnamese chars stripped via unicodedata).
acceptance_criteria: normalize_number('1.000.000', locale='vi') returns 1000000.0
normalize_number('1,000,000', locale='en') returns 1000000.0
normalize_number('1,500.50', locale='en') returns 1500.50
normalize_date('01/05/2025') returns '2025-05-01'
normalize_date('2025-01-15') returns '2025-01-15'
normalize_date('not-a-date') returns None
normalize_currency('VND 500.000') returns (500000.0, 'VND')
normalize_currency('$50.00') returns (50.0, 'USD')
to_snake_case('Transaction Date') returns 'transaction_date'
to_snake_case('Amount (VND)') returns 'amount_vnd'
edge_cases: normalize_number on already-numeric float/int should return it unchanged
detect_locale with fewer than 5 non-null values defaults to 'en'
normalize_date must not accept '99/99/9999' as valid
to_snake_case must handle consecutive spaces and multiple hyphens

tasks.name: Layer 1 — File Detection & Parsing
tasks.description: Create ave/pipeline/layer1_ingestion.py partial implementation (file detection + parsing only, normalization in T014). Implement _detect_format(file_path: Path) -> str — check extension first (.csv -> 'csv', .xlsx/.xls -> 'xlsx'), then magic bytes (read first 4 bytes: PK\x03\x04 = xlsx). Raise IngestionError for unsupported formats. Implement _detect_encoding(file_path: Path) -> Tuple[str, float] — use chardet.detect on first 100KB, return (encoding, confidence). Implement _detect_separator(file_path: Path, encoding: str) -> str — read first 5 lines, test each separator [',', ';', '\t', '|'] via csv.Sniffer and column count consistency, return best separator. Implement _parse_file(file_path: Path, config: IngestionConfig) -> Tuple[DataFrame, dict] — for CSV: call pandas.read_csv with detected encoding, separator, header_row, skip_rows; for Excel: call pandas.read_excel with openpyxl engine, sheet_name, header. Return (raw_df, parse_meta dict with encoding_used, separator_used, sheet_used, rows_raw, parse_warnings list).
acceptance_criteria: _detect_format raises IngestionError with helpful message for .pdf files
_detect_encoding returns ('utf-8', confidence) for UTF-8 file
_detect_encoding falls back to 'utf-8' with warning when confidence < 0.7
_detect_separator correctly identifies tab-separated file
_parse_file with Excel file and default sheet reads first sheet
_parse_file with CSV with BOM encoding parses correctly
edge_cases: File extension is .csv but content is Excel binary: _detect_format must check magic bytes
Excel file with password protection: openpyxl raises exception — catch and raise IngestionError
CSV with inconsistent column counts: use on_bad_lines='warn' and log count of bad rows
Empty file (0 bytes): raise IngestionError before attempting parse

tasks.name: Layer 1 — Schema Normalization & Manifest Creation
tasks.description: Extend ave/pipeline/layer1_ingestion.py. Implement _prune_empty(df: DataFrame) -> Tuple[DataFrame, int, int] — drops fully-null rows and fully-null columns, returns (pruned_df, rows_dropped, cols_dropped). Implement _normalize_headers(df: DataFrame) -> Tuple[DataFrame, dict] — strips whitespace, applies to_snake_case, resolves duplicates (append _1, _2), returns (df, {original: normalized} mapping). Implement _infer_schema(df: DataFrame, config: AveConfig) -> List[ColumnMeta] — for each column, detect dtype using heuristics and any column type hints from config. Implement _normalize_values(df: DataFrame, schema: List[ColumnMeta], locale: str) -> Tuple[DataFrame, List[str]] — apply per-column normalization based on inferred dtype; returns (normalized_df, list of warnings). Implement _create_manifest(file_path, parse_meta, schema, df_raw, df_normalized, session_id) -> SourceManifest. Implement _switch_to_polars_if_large(df: DataFrame, threshold: int) -> Any — converts Pandas DataFrame to Polars if row count >= threshold. Assemble full run(ctx: PipelineContext) -> PipelineContext function following Section 2.1.3 steps 1-10, writing trail entries at FILE_RECEIVED, SCHEMA_NORMALIZED, MANIFEST_CREATED.
acceptance_criteria: _prune_empty removes rows where ALL cells are NaN
_normalize_headers converts 'Transaction Date' to 'transaction_date'
_normalize_headers resolves 'amount', 'amount' to 'amount', 'amount_1'
_infer_schema correctly identifies date, numeric, currency, text columns
run() sets ctx.normalized_df, ctx.source_manifest, ctx.ingestion_report
run() writes exactly 3 trail entries: FILE_RECEIVED, SCHEMA_NORMALIZED, MANIFEST_CREATED
Polars used when row count >= config.pipeline.use_polars_threshold
edge_cases: Column with 60% dates and 40% text should be inferred as 'mixed' dtype
After header normalization, if a normalized name is a Python keyword (e.g. 'type'), append underscore: 'type_'
_normalize_values must not modify the original raw_df — work on a copy

tasks.name: Rule YAML DSL — Loader & Validator
tasks.description: Create ave/engines/rule_engine.py partial (Rule model + loader/validator only, evaluator in T016). Define Rule dataclass with all fields from Section 2.3.3: id, name, description, field, fields, condition, threshold, value, values, pattern, reference_field, severity, requires_cross_check, active, tags, audit_standard. Implement load_rules_from_yaml(path: Path) -> List[Rule] — parses YAML, validates each rule, raises RuleValidationError with rule id and field name for any violation. Implement validate_rules(rules: List[Rule]) -> List[str] — returns list of error strings (empty = valid). Validation checks: id matches ^[A-Z][0-9]{3}$; severity is high|medium|low; condition is a recognized type (all types from Section 2.3.3); duplicate IDs raise error; threshold required when condition is gt/gte/lt/lte; pattern required when condition is matches/not_matches; field required for single-field conditions; compound conditions require logic (AND|OR) and sub_conditions list.
inputs: Section 2.3.3 Rule YAML DSL full spec
acceptance_criteria: load_rules_from_yaml('default_rules.yaml') loads all 20 rules without error
Rule with duplicate ID raises RuleValidationError mentioning the ID
Rule with condition='gt' and no threshold raises RuleValidationError
Rule with active=false is loaded but marked inactive
validate_rules on valid list returns empty list
Missing required field 'id' raises RuleValidationError with position info
edge_cases: YAML file with empty rules list returns [] (valid — pipeline warns but continues)
Rule with fields: [] (empty list) should raise validation error
compound rule with missing logic field raises RuleValidationError
Rule ID case sensitivity: 'r001' does not match ^[A-Z][0-9]{3}$ — raise error

tasks.name: Rule Engine — Condition Evaluators
tasks.description: Extend ave/engines/rule_engine.py with all condition evaluators. Implement evaluate_rule(rule: Rule, df: Any) -> List[Finding] using a match statement dispatching on rule.condition. Implement each evaluator as a private function: _eval_gt/_eval_gte/_eval_lt/_eval_lte (numeric comparison, handle None/NaN by not flagging); _eval_eq/_eval_neq (equality, type-coerced); _eval_in/_eval_not_in (membership in values list); _eval_is_null/_eval_not_null (null check); _eval_matches/_eval_not_matches (regex, compile pattern once with re.compile); _eval_weekend_transaction(field, df) -> flags rows where the date column falls on Saturday (weekday()==5) or Sunday (weekday()==6); _eval_end_of_period(field, df, config) -> flags dates that are last day of month/quarter/year per config; _eval_cross_field_gt/_eval_cross_field_eq (compare two columns); _eval_compound(rule, df) -> applies sub_conditions with AND/OR logic. Each evaluator must return List[Finding] where each Finding has: finding_type='anomaly', layer=3, detection_method='rule', rule_id=rule.id, rule_name=rule.name, row_index (0-based), row_data=row.to_dict(), severity=rule.severity, confidence=1.0, reasoning='<Rule name>: <specific value and field>'. Implement evaluate(df: Any) -> List[Finding] that runs all active rules and deduplicates by (rule_id, row_index).
inputs: T012 normalization (for date parsing in weekend check)
acceptance_criteria: gt evaluator on amount column flags rows where amount > threshold
weekend_transaction evaluator correctly identifies Saturday and Sunday
compound AND evaluator only flags rows where ALL sub-conditions match
compound OR evaluator flags rows where ANY sub-condition matches
Rule referencing non-existent column logs warning and returns [] (not exception)
matches evaluator with invalid regex raises RuleValidationError at load time
evaluate() deduplicates: same rule + same row produces exactly 1 Finding
Reasoning string includes the specific column value that triggered the rule
edge_cases: gt evaluator on null value: null should NOT be flagged (only numeric comparison)
weekend_transaction on a non-date column: log warning, skip rule
end_of_period for last day of fiscal year (Dec 31): correctly identifies 31st as end
cross_field_gt where reference_field is null: do not flag

tasks.name: Default Rules YAML File (20 rules)
tasks.description: Create rules/default_rules.yaml containing all 20 rules from Section 2.3.4 in the DSL format defined in Section 2.3.3. Each rule must be a complete, valid entry: id, name, description, field(s), condition, threshold/value/values/pattern as appropriate, severity, requires_cross_check, active: true, tags, audit_standard. Rules requiring configurable thresholds must use the config's approval_thresholds values as comments but default to the values in Section 2.3.4. R006 uses fiscal_year_end_month/day from config. R007 requires approved_vendor_file config (if null, skip rule with warning). R015 requires public_holidays list from config (if empty, skip with warning). Also create rules/examples/vas_rules.yaml and rules/examples/isa_rules.yaml as minimal examples with 2-3 rules each tagged with the respective audit standard.
acceptance_criteria: load_rules_from_yaml('rules/default_rules.yaml') loads exactly 20 rules
All 20 rule IDs match R001-R020 in order
validate_rules returns empty list for default_rules.yaml
R002 uses compound condition with weekend_transaction and is_null
R009 uses compound condition with gte and lte on amount field
R018 (frequent small transactions) uses a valid DSL condition or is marked as requiring special handling via tag
edge_cases: R018 (frequent small transactions — same vendor, same day, count > 5) cannot be expressed purely in per-row DSL — mark active: false with a comment explaining it needs post-aggregation logic, implement as a special case in Layer 3
R007 and R015 must have active: true but the evaluator skips them gracefully when config values are absent

tasks.name: Layer 2 — Integrity Agent (IC-001 to IC-005)
tasks.description: Create ave/pipeline/layer2_integrity.py partial. Implement the first 5 integrity checks as private functions that each accept (df: Any, config: AveConfig) -> List[Finding]: _check_ic001_null_completeness(df, config) — for each column, if null_ratio > threshold (from config per-column or default), create Finding with finding_type='integrity', layer=2, rule_id='IC-001', row_index of each null row, column_name, severity based on whether column is required; _check_ic002_duplicate_rows(df, config) — hash each row (all columns as string tuple), find hashes appearing > 1 time, flag all duplicate rows (not just the second occurrence), severity='high'; _check_ic003_duplicate_keys(df, config) — if config.primary_key_columns is non-empty, find rows where key tuple appears > 1 time, severity='high'; _check_ic004_date_range(df, config) — for all date-typed columns, flag rows outside [min_date, max_date] from config, severity='medium'; _check_ic005_numeric_range(df, config) — for columns with min_value/max_value in config.columns, flag out-of-range values, severity='medium'. Each Finding must include: specific value that failed, expected constraint in reasoning field.
acceptance_criteria: IC-001 flags row 5 when row 5 has null in a required column
IC-002 flags all instances of duplicate rows (row 2 AND row 7 if they're identical)
IC-003 does nothing and returns [] when primary_key_columns is empty list
IC-004 flags a date of '1800-01-01' as out of range
IC-005 flags a row where amount = -1 when min_value = 0 for that column
All findings include actual_value and expected constraint in reasoning
edge_cases: IC-002: row hashing must handle NaN values consistently (treat as fixed sentinel 'NULL_VALUE')
IC-004: column inferred as 'mixed' dtype should attempt date parse on string values before checking range
IC-001: a column with 0 required annotation and 0 nulls should produce no findings

tasks.name: Layer 2 — Integrity Agent (IC-006 to IC-010)
tasks.description: Continue ave/pipeline/layer2_integrity.py. Implement remaining 5 checks: _check_ic006_negative_amounts(df, config) — for currency-typed columns not configured to allow negatives, flag rows with negative values, severity='medium'; _check_ic007_format_consistency(df, config) — for columns with pattern defined in config.columns, compile regex once and flag non-matching non-null values, severity='low'; _check_ic008_referential_integrity(df, config) — if foreign_key mappings in config, load reference set from config-specified file or list, flag values not in set, severity='high'; _check_ic009_cross_column_logic(df, config) — evaluate cross-column expressions from config (e.g. end_date >= start_date), flag violations, severity='medium'; _check_ic010_statistical_outliers(df, config) — for numeric columns, compute mean and std, flag values beyond mean ± N*std (default N=3 from config), severity='low'. Implement _score_findings(findings: List[Finding]) -> dict — computes total_severity_score and overall_rating. Implement full run(ctx: PipelineContext) -> PipelineContext that calls all 10 checks, scores findings, saves integrity_report and integrity_findings to context, writes trail entry with action_type='INTEGRITY_CHECK_RUN'.
acceptance_criteria: IC-010 flags a value of 9999999 when all other values are between 100-1000 (clearly an outlier)
_score_findings returns overall_rating='SEVERE' when total score > 30
_score_findings returns overall_rating='CLEAN' when findings list is empty
run() writes exactly 1 trail entry with action_type='INTEGRITY_CHECK_RUN'
run() sets ctx.integrity_report with all required keys from Section 2.2
IC-006 does NOT flag columns where config explicitly allows negatives
edge_cases: IC-010: column with fewer than 10 rows should skip outlier detection (insufficient sample)
IC-008: if reference file doesn't exist, log warning and skip check (don't crash)
IC-009: expression evaluation must catch ZeroDivisionError and log warning per-row

tasks.name: Layer 3 — Rule-Based Anomaly Detection
tasks.description: Create ave/pipeline/layer3_anomaly.py partial (rule-based only). Implement run_rule_engine(ctx: PipelineContext) -> List[Finding]: instantiates RuleEngine with rules from ctx.config, calls evaluate(ctx.normalized_df), logs per-rule finding counts at DEBUG level, writes a trail entry per rule that produced >= 1 finding (action_type='ANOMALY_RULE_EVALUATED', reasoning_summary includes rule_id and count). Also implement the R018 special case (frequency-based): _check_r018_frequent_small_transactions(df, config) — group by (vendor_id column if exists + date column), count transactions per group, flag all rows in groups where count > 5 and all amounts < approval_thresholds.level_1. If required columns don't exist, log warning and return []. Write trail entry for R018 results. Combine rule engine findings + R018 findings. Write single summary trail entry with action_type='ANOMALY_RULE_EVALUATED' and total count.
acceptance_criteria: Findings from rule engine are stored in ctx.anomaly_findings
R018 check returns findings when same vendor has 6+ small transactions on same day
R018 returns [] without error when vendor_id column does not exist
Trail entries are written for each rule that produced findings
Rule findings have detection_method='rule' and confidence=1.0
edge_cases: If all 20 rules produce 0 findings, anomaly_findings is empty list (not None)
R018 must handle DataFrames where vendor column name varies (look for column name containing 'vendor')

tasks.name: LLM Client — Ollama Implementation
tasks.description: Create ave/engines/llm_client.py with the LLMClient abstract base class from Section 5.2 and the OllamaClient implementation. LLMClient ABC: complete(prompt, system_prompt) -> str; complete_json(prompt, system_prompt, max_retries) -> dict; health_check() -> bool. OllamaClient.__init__(model: str, base_url: str = 'http://localhost:11434', timeout: int = 30) — reads base_url from AVE_OLLAMA_URL env var if set. complete() — POST to /api/generate with model, prompt, stream=false, options={temperature: config.temperature}. Use httpx.Client with timeout. Parse response JSON, extract response field. complete_json() — calls complete() with explicit JSON instruction appended to prompt; attempts json.loads() on response; strips markdown code fences before parsing; on JSONDecodeError, retries up to max_retries with prompt 'You must respond with ONLY valid JSON, no other text:' prefixed. health_check() — GET /api/tags, returns True if 200, False otherwise. Wrap all httpx exceptions in LLMUnavailableError.
acceptance_criteria: health_check() returns False (not exception) when Ollama is not running
complete_json() succeeds when LLM wraps JSON in markdown code fences
complete_json() raises LLMResponseError after max_retries on persistent bad JSON
Timeout raises LLMUnavailableError with provider='ollama' in message
AVE_OLLAMA_URL environment variable overrides default localhost URL
edge_cases: LLM response with BOM character at start of JSON string
LLM response that is valid JSON but empty object {} should be returned without error
Connection refused (Ollama not installed): raise LLMUnavailableError with install instructions

tasks.name: LLM Client — Groq & Mistral Implementations
tasks.description: Extend ave/engines/llm_client.py. Implement GroqClient(LLMClient): __init__(model: str, api_key: str = None) — api_key defaults to os.environ.get('GROQ_API_KEY'); raises LLMUnavailableError at init if api_key is None. complete() — POST to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions with OpenAI-compatible format (messages array with system + user roles). health_check() — attempt a minimal completion with 1 max_token. Implement MistralClient(LLMClient): similar to GroqClient but uses MISTRAL_API_KEY and https://api.mistral.ai/v1/chat/completions. Implement LLMRouter: __init__(primary: str, config: LLMConfig) — builds fallback chain [primary, next_in_chain, ..., 'none'] where chain order is ollama -> groq -> mistral. get_client() -> Optional[LLMClient] — returns first available client (health_check passes). If no client available, returns None. complete_with_fallback(prompt, system_prompt) -> Optional[str] — tries clients in order, returns first successful response, None if all fail.
acceptance_criteria: GroqClient raises LLMUnavailableError at init when GROQ_API_KEY not set
LLMRouter with primary='ollama' tries Groq if Ollama health_check fails
LLMRouter.complete_with_fallback returns None when all providers unavailable
Both GroqClient and MistralClient use OpenAI-compatible message format
Router logs which provider was used at DEBUG level
edge_cases: Groq API rate limit (HTTP 429): catch and include in error message, do not retry
Mistral API returns 503: treat as unavailable, try next in fallback chain
LLMRouter with primary='none': immediately returns None from complete_with_fallback

tasks.name: Layer 3 — LLM-Assisted Anomaly Detection
tasks.description: Extend ave/pipeline/layer3_anomaly.py with LLM mode. Implement _prepare_llm_batches(df: Any, already_flagged_indices: Set[int], batch_size: int) -> List[List[dict]] — excludes already-flagged rows, strips PII columns per config, serializes remaining rows to dicts in batches of batch_size. Implement _build_anomaly_prompt(batch: List[dict], column_defs: dict, audit_context: str, flagged: Set[int]) -> str using the template from Section 2.3.5 exactly. Implement _parse_llm_findings(response: dict, batch_offset: int, df: Any, session_id: str) -> List[Finding] — extracts flagged list from response, validates each: row_index is integer within [0, len(df)), confidence in [0,1], severity is valid. Cross-validates: for each flagged item, verify that the LLM-stated evidence (the row_index) actually contains the values described in reasoning by checking df.iloc[row_index]. Discard any finding where evidence cannot be verified. Create Finding with detection_method='llm'. Implement run_llm_detection(ctx: PipelineContext) -> List[Finding] — calls LLMRouter, batches, parses, writes trail entries for each LLM call (LLM_QUERY_SENT, LLM_RESPONSE_RECEIVED). If LLM unavailable, log warning and return []. Assemble complete run(ctx) combining rule-based + LLM findings.
acceptance_criteria: LLM findings have detection_method='llm' and confidence from LLM response
Finding with row_index=99 in a 50-row DataFrame is discarded
LLM findings are excluded for rows already flagged by rule engine
Trail entries LLM_QUERY_SENT and LLM_RESPONSE_RECEIVED written per batch
If LLM returns empty flagged list, no findings added (not an error)
PII columns are not included in LLM batch payload
edge_cases: LLM returns row_index as a string '5' instead of integer: coerce to int
LLM returns duplicate row_index entries in same response: deduplicate
LLM call timeout mid-batch: log warning, discard that batch, continue with next

tasks.name: Layer 4 — Cross-Verification Stub
tasks.description: Create ave/pipeline/layer4_crossverify.py as the MVP stub implementation per Section 2.4.1. Implement run(ctx: PipelineContext) -> PipelineContext: copies ctx.anomaly_findings + ctx.integrity_findings into ctx.verified_findings with cross_verified=False and cross_verify_status='pending' on each finding (create new Finding instances — do not mutate originals). Write one trail entry with action_type='CROSS_VERIFY_RUN', reasoning_summary='Cross-verification skipped in MVP — findings passed through unchanged'. Log INFO: 'Layer 4 (Cross-Verification): stub mode — {len(findings)} findings passed through'. Return context. Also add a module-level comment: # TODO: Full cross-verification implementation in Alpha (Section 2.4.2)
acceptance_criteria: run() sets ctx.verified_findings to combined list from anomaly_findings + integrity_findings
Each finding in verified_findings has cross_verified=False
Trail entry is written with action_type='CROSS_VERIFY_RUN'
Original findings in ctx.anomaly_findings and ctx.integrity_findings are not mutated
edge_cases: Empty findings list: verified_findings = [] (not None)
Finding with no finding_id should be assigned a UUID before storing in verified_findings

tasks.name: Layer 5 — Findings Aggregation & JSON Report
tasks.description: Create ave/pipeline/layer5_synthesis.py partial (aggregation + JSON report). Implement _aggregate_findings(ctx: PipelineContext) -> dict — builds the final_report dict matching the JSON schema from Section 2.5.2. Includes: report_meta (session_id, generated_at, ave_version, file_processed, config_file, config_hash), source_summary (from manifest), integrity_summary (overall_rating, score, check counts, finding counts), anomaly_summary (total, by_severity dict, by_rule dict, llm_assisted_count), findings (serialized list of all Finding objects via to_dict()), audit_trail_ref (trail file path), recommendations (empty list at this stage — filled in T026). Implement _export_json(report: dict, output_dir: Path, session_id: str) -> Path — writes report to {output_dir}/{session_id}_report.json with indent=2. Returns file path. Implement partial run(ctx) that aggregates and writes JSON.
acceptance_criteria: JSON report matches schema from Section 2.5.2 exactly
anomaly_summary.by_severity counts match actual findings
anomaly_summary.llm_assisted_count correctly counts detection_method='llm' findings
JSON file is valid and parseable
report_meta.config_hash is SHA-256 of config file content
edge_cases: If source_manifest is None (ingestion failed), report_meta still writes with available data
Findings list may be empty: anomaly_summary.total_flagged = 0 is valid

tasks.name: Layer 5 — Markdown Report & Recommendations
tasks.description: Create ave/export/markdown_exporter.py. Implement MarkdownExporter class with generate(report: dict, ctx: PipelineContext) -> str — produces Markdown string matching the template in Section 2.5.2. Sections: # AVE Audit Report header with metadata, ## Executive Summary (2-3 sentence text — LLM-generated if enabled, template-based if not), ## Integrity Check Results (table with IC-001 to IC-010 pass/fail and finding counts), ## Anomaly Findings (table: Row Index | Rule ID | Description | Severity | Value), ## High-Severity Findings Detail (one sub-section per high finding with full evidence), ## Audit Trail Reference (path + hash). Implement _generate_recommendations_template(findings: List) -> List[str] — rules-based: if >3 high findings, add 'Escalate to senior auditor'; if any IC-002 duplicates, add 'Investigate source system for duplicate entries'; etc. (5 template rules). Implement _generate_recommendations_llm(findings_summary: str, llm_client) -> List[str] — sends prompt asking for 3-5 actionable audit recommendations. Export MarkdownExporter, update Layer 5 run() to call it and write {session_id}_report.md.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Markdown has all 5 required sections
Findings table has correct column headers: Row Index | Rule ID | Description | Severity | Value
Template recommendations appear when LLM disabled
LLM recommendations appear when LLM enabled and available
Empty findings produces Markdown report with 'No anomalies detected' in findings section
edge_cases: Findings table with > 100 rows: include top 50 by severity and add note 'and N more findings in JSON report'
Markdown special characters in row data (e.g. pipe |) must be escaped in table cells

tasks.name: Layer 5 — PDF Export
tasks.description: Create ave/export/pdf_exporter.py. Implement PdfExporter class. __init__: check if weasyprint is importable; set self.available = True/False. generate(markdown_content: str, output_path: Path, session_id: str) -> Optional[Path]: if not available, log warning 'PDF export skipped: WeasyPrint not installed' and return None. Convert Markdown to HTML using Python markdown library (pip install markdown). Wrap HTML in a complete HTML document with embedded CSS: Arial font, A4 page size, table styling, header with 'AVE Audit Report' and session ID, footer with page number and SHA-256 hash of report content. Use weasyprint.HTML(string=html_content).write_pdf(output_path). Return output_path. Update Layer 5 run() to call PDF export if 'pdf' in config.output.formats. Write trail entry with action_type='EXPORT_COMPLETED' including format='pdf' and file path.
acceptance_criteria: generate() returns None without exception when WeasyPrint not installed
PDF is generated when WeasyPrint available
PDF contains page numbers in footer
PDF has AVE header with session ID on each page
Hash of report content appears in PDF footer
edge_cases: WeasyPrint raises error due to missing Cairo/Pango system libraries: catch and return None with detailed error message
Markdown with code blocks: render as monospace in PDF
Very long findings table: PDF pagination must not split table rows

tasks.name: Complete Layer 5 — Trail Finalization & DB Save
tasks.description: Complete ave/pipeline/layer5_synthesis.py run() function. After all exports, call ctx.trail_writer.finalize() to write the CHAIN_HASH entry and get trail file path. Save final session record to SQLite via ctx.database.update_session() with: completed_at=now, status='completed', overall_rating, total_findings count. Save all findings via ctx.database.save_finding() for each finding in ctx.verified_findings. Save rule snapshot via ctx.database.save_rule_snapshot(). Write final trail entry with action_type='EXPORT_COMPLETED' containing all exported file paths. Update ctx.report_paths with {format: path} for all generated reports. Set ctx.final_report. Return ctx.
acceptance_criteria: finalize() called exactly once per session
SQLite session record updated with completed_at and status='completed'
All verified_findings saved to SQLite findings table
ctx.report_paths contains paths for all generated formats
Trail CHAIN_HASH entry is the last line in the JSONL file
edge_cases: If database.save_finding() fails for one finding, continue with others and log error (don't abort)
If trail finalize() fails, log StorageError but still update database

tasks.name: LangGraph Orchestrator
tasks.description: Create ave/orchestrator.py. Import all 5 layer run() functions. Implement build_pipeline() -> CompiledGraph using LangGraph StateGraph(PipelineContext): add_node for each of 5 layers; set_entry_point('ingest'); add_edge chain ingest->integrity->anomaly->crossverify->synthesize->END; add_conditional_edges from 'ingest' to either 'integrity' (if not ctx.has_fatal_error()) or END. Add error-handling wrapper around each node: if layer raises PipelineError, call ctx.add_error(str(e)), continue to next layer with partial context (all layers must be resilient). Implement run_pipeline(config: AveConfig, file_path: Path, output_dir: Path, session_id: str, trail_writer: TrailWriter, db: AveDatabase, llm_client: Optional[LLMClient]) -> PipelineContext: initializes PipelineContext, invokes compiled pipeline, handles checkpoint resume logic (check_checkpoint, ask user, load or start fresh), returns final context. Implement _ask_resume(session_id: str) -> bool — uses rich.prompt.Confirm if stdin is TTY, returns True automatically if non-interactive.
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline executes layers in order: 1->2->3->4->5
Fatal ingestion error causes pipeline to skip to END without running layers 2-5
Layer 3 exception does not abort pipeline — layer 4 and 5 run with partial findings
Checkpoint resume prompts user when incomplete session found
_ask_resume returns True without prompting in non-TTY environments
edge_cases: LangGraph version mismatch: catch ImportError and provide installation instructions
Two incomplete sessions with same file: prompt must show session ID to disambiguate

tasks.name: CLI — `ave run` Command
tasks.description: Create ave/cli.py. Initialize Typer app = typer.Typer(name='ave', help='Autonomous Verification Engine for Auditors'). Implement run command with all options from Section 2.7.2: file (Path, required), config (Path, default='./audit_rules.yaml'), output_dir (Path, default='./ave_output/'), llm (str, default='ollama'), llm_model (Optional[str]), sheet (Optional[str]), verbose (bool flag), dry_run (bool flag), no_llm (bool flag), report_format (str, default='all'). Implementation: (1) Call setup_logging(verbose); (2) Generate session_id = str(uuid4()); (3) Load config via load_config(); (4) Check LLM availability via LLMRouter; (5) Initialize TrailWriter, AveDatabase; (6) Call run_pipeline(); (7) Display summary using rich.console: session ID, file processed, overall_rating, finding counts by severity, generated report paths; (8) Exit with appropriate exit code (Section 2.7.3). Handle all AveError subclasses with specific error messages and correct exit codes.
acceptance_criteria: ave run --help shows all options with descriptions
ave run --file nonexistent.csv exits with code 1 and helpful message
ave run --no-llm equivalent to --llm none
Successful run exits with code 0
Summary displayed after run shows session ID, rating, and report paths
dry_run flag completes pipeline but writes no output files
edge_cases: --file with relative path: resolve to absolute before passing to pipeline
--config not found: exit code 1 with message 'Config file not found: {path}'
KeyboardInterrupt during run: write INTERRUPTED trail entry, exit with code 2

tasks.name: CLI — `ave review` Interactive Command
tasks.description: Extend ave/cli.py. Implement review command: ave review --session <session_id>. Loads findings from SQLite for session. Uses rich.console to display findings one by one: show row data in a table, rule triggered, severity badge (color-coded: red=high, yellow=medium, blue=low), reasoning. Prompt user for each finding: [A]ccept / [R]eject / [S]kip / [Q]uit. On A/R: write HUMAN_REVIEW_RECEIVED trail entry with human_decision='accepted'|'rejected'; update finding in SQLite with human_decision, human_decision_at, human_decision_note. On S: skip without writing. On Q: exit review mode with summary of decisions made. At end, display summary: N accepted, M rejected, K skipped. If session not found: exit with code 1. If session has no findings: display 'No findings to review for session {id}'.
acceptance_criteria: Each finding shown with color-coded severity
Accept decision updates SQLite finding record with human_decision='accepted'
Trail entry HUMAN_REVIEW_RECEIVED written for each accept/reject
Quit mid-review saves all decisions made so far
Running review twice on same session shows updated decision status
edge_cases: Terminal resize during review: rich handles responsively
Session with 200 findings: paginate at 10 per screen
Non-TTY environment (piped input): raise error 'Review requires interactive terminal'

tasks.name: CLI — Utility Commands
tasks.description: Extend ave/cli.py with remaining commands. ave export --session <id> --format <pdf|json|markdown>: loads session from DB, regenerates requested report format from stored findings, writes to output_dir. ave sessions --last N: queries SQLite for last N sessions, displays as rich Table: session_id, created_at, file, status, overall_rating, findings_count. ave validate-config --config <path>: loads YAML, runs validation, displays either 'Config valid: N rules loaded' or table of validation errors with rule ID and error message. ave check-llm --provider <ollama|groq|mistral>: runs health_check() on specified provider, displays status + model list for Ollama (GET /api/tags), API connectivity for Groq/Mistral. Each command follows exit codes from Section 2.7.3.
acceptance_criteria: ave sessions --last 5 shows 5 most recent sessions in table
ave validate-config on default_rules.yaml shows '20 rules loaded'
ave check-llm --provider ollama shows list of available Ollama models
ave export regenerates report without re-running pipeline
All commands have --help documentation
edge_cases: ave export for session with status='running': display warning 'Session incomplete'
ave sessions on empty database: display 'No sessions found'
ave check-llm --provider groq without GROQ_API_KEY: show 'API key not set' message

tasks.name: Progress Bar & Rich Console Integration
tasks.description: Create ave/utils/progress.py. Implement PipelineProgress class using rich.progress.Progress with SpinnerColumn, TextColumn, BarColumn, TimeElapsedColumn. Steps: Layer 1 Ingestion (weight: 10%), Layer 2 Integrity (weight: 20%), Layer 3 Anomaly Detection (weight: 40%), Layer 4 Cross-Verification (weight: 10%), Layer 5 Synthesis (weight: 20%). Implement start(total_steps=5), update(layer: int, message: str), complete(layer: int), error(layer: int, message: str). Progress must display: current layer name, row count being processed, elapsed time. Also implement print_summary(ctx: PipelineContext) using rich.table.Table and rich.panel.Panel: show file processed, session ID, overall rating (color-coded panel: green=CLEAN, yellow=MINOR/MODERATE, red=SEVERE), findings table by severity, report file paths. Integrate progress updates into orchestrator (pass progress object through context or as callback).
acceptance_criteria: Progress bar shows percentage completion based on current layer
Overall rating panel is green for CLEAN, red for SEVERE
Elapsed time shown next to current layer
print_summary shows correct finding counts per severity
Progress renders correctly in 80-column terminal
edge_cases: Non-TTY terminal (e.g. CI environment): disable rich progress, use plain text logging only
Pipeline error in layer: mark that layer as failed in progress (red X)

tasks.name: Test Fixtures — CSV & Config Files
tasks.description: Create all test fixture files in tests/fixtures/. Generate programmatically using Python scripts to ensure determinism. Files to create: clean_transactions.csv (100 rows, columns: id, transaction_date, amount, vendor_id, description, approver, currency — all valid values); transactions_with_nulls.csv (100 rows, 10 rows have null in 'approver' and 'amount' fields); transactions_with_duplicates.csv (100 rows, rows 20-24 are exact copies of rows 10-14); transactions_with_anomalies.csv (500 rows, contains violations for R001-R020 including: 5 transactions > 500M VND, 3 weekend transactions without approval, 10 round-number transactions, 2 duplicate invoice numbers, amounts just below approval threshold); malformed_encoding.csv (50 rows, saved as latin-1); excel_single_sheet.xlsx (200 rows, same schema); excel_multi_sheet.xlsx (3 sheets, 200/100/50 rows). Config files: minimal_config.yaml (only required fields), full_config.yaml (all fields including approval thresholds, date ranges, column types), invalid_rules.yaml (contains duplicate ID, missing threshold, bad condition type). Write a generate_fixtures.py script that recreates all fixtures deterministically.
acceptance_criteria: transactions_with_anomalies.csv triggers at least 15 unique rules when processed
transactions_with_duplicates.csv has exactly 5 duplicate row pairs (10 flagged rows)
malformed_encoding.csv is detected as latin-1 with chardet confidence > 0.7
excel_multi_sheet.xlsx has exactly 3 sheets named 'Sheet1', 'Sheet2', 'Sheet3'
generate_fixtures.py recreates identical files on re-run (deterministic random seed)
edge_cases: transactions_with_anomalies.csv: include at least one row that triggers R009 (amount between 90%-100% of threshold)
full_config.yaml: must reference default_rules.yaml as rules_file

tasks.name: Unit Tests — Normalization Utilities
tasks.description: Create tests/unit/test_normalization.py. Test all functions in ave/utils/normalization.py. Test cases for normalize_number: Vietnamese format '1.000.000' -> 1000000.0, US format '1,000,000' -> 1000000.0, mixed '1,500.50' -> 1500.50, with currency prefix '₫ 500.000' -> 500000.0, already-float 1500.0 -> 1500.0, empty string -> None, 'N/A' -> None. Test cases for normalize_date: all 5 format patterns, invalid dates return None, '99/99/9999' returns None, datetime objects pass through. Test cases for detect_locale: series with Vietnamese dots returns 'vi', series with English commas returns 'en', mixed series returns best guess. Test to_snake_case: spaces, hyphens, Vietnamese diacritics stripped, consecutive separators, Python keywords. Test strip_pii_if_needed: specified columns become '[REDACTED]', other columns unchanged. Use pytest.mark.parametrize for multiple test cases.
acceptance_criteria: All test cases pass
pytest --cov=ave/utils/normalization.py shows >= 90% line coverage
No tests depend on external files or network

tasks.name: Unit Tests — Hashing & Trail Writer
tasks.description: Create tests/unit/test_hashing.py and tests/unit/test_trail_writer.py. In test_hashing.py: test hash_file on known content returns expected SHA-256; test hash_dict is order-independent (same keys different insertion order); test hash_dataframe is deterministic; test verify_hash returns False on tampered string. In test_trail_writer.py: test write() appends correct JSONL line; test that JSONL file is not overwritten on second write(); test finalize() writes CHAIN_HASH entry as last line; test verify() returns True on untampered file; test verify() returns False when one character is changed in a preceding entry; test that write() after finalize() raises exception; test file permissions are 0o600 on creation (skip on Windows).
acceptance_criteria: All tests pass
Trail tamper test uses actual file modification (not mocking)
Permissions test is platform-conditional (skip on Windows)
>= 95% coverage on hashing.py and trail_writer.py

tasks.name: Unit Tests — Rule Engine
tasks.description: Create tests/unit/test_rule_engine.py. Test load_rules_from_yaml with valid file; test validate_rules on rules with each type of violation; test each condition evaluator type with: (a) row that matches, (b) row that does not match, (c) row with null in target column. Use parametrize for condition types: gt, gte, lt, lte, eq, neq, in, not_in, is_null, not_null, matches, not_matches, weekend_transaction, end_of_period, cross_field_gt, cross_field_eq, compound AND, compound OR. Test evaluate() deduplication. Test Rule with active=false produces no findings. Test rule referencing missing column returns [] with warning logged. Test compound rule with AND where one sub-condition is null-producing column. Test R002 equivalent (compound weekend + missing approval) triggers correctly on Saturday transaction.
acceptance_criteria: All condition evaluator tests pass
compound AND test with null sub-condition produces no finding (not exception)
>= 90% coverage on rule_engine.py
Each finding from evaluate() has non-empty reasoning string

tasks.name: Unit Tests — Integrity Agent
tasks.description: Create tests/unit/test_integrity.py. For each of 10 integrity checks (IC-001 to IC-010): write at least 2 tests — one that produces findings, one that produces none (clean data). Additional tests: IC-001 with configured null threshold of 50% (should not flag 30% null ratio); IC-002 detects exact duplicate rows but not rows with single different value; IC-003 with empty primary_key_columns returns no findings; IC-010 with fewer than 10 rows returns no findings (insufficient sample); _score_findings with empty list returns CLEAN; _score_findings with 12 high-severity findings returns SEVERE; overall_rating transitions: 0=CLEAN, 5=MINOR, 20=MODERATE, 35=SEVERE.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 IC tests pass with both clean and dirty data
IC-010 insufficient sample test passes
Score transitions test all 4 ratings
>= 90% coverage on layer2_integrity.py

tasks.name: Unit Tests — LLM Client
tasks.description: Create tests/unit/test_llm_client.py. Use pytest-mock to mock httpx.Client. Test OllamaClient.health_check() returns False when mock returns connection error; returns True when mock returns 200. Test OllamaClient.complete_json() with mock returning valid JSON; with mock returning JSON in markdown code fences; with mock returning invalid JSON triggers retry; after max_retries raises LLMResponseError. Test GroqClient raises LLMUnavailableError at init when GROQ_API_KEY not in env. Test LLMRouter with primary='ollama' and Ollama returning unavailable: falls back to Groq if GROQ_API_KEY set. Test LLMRouter.complete_with_fallback returns None when all providers fail. Use environment variable patching with monkeypatch.
acceptance_criteria: All tests use mocks — no real HTTP calls
Retry behavior tested with mock returning bad JSON exactly N times
GroqClient init test works without GROQ_API_KEY in environment
>= 85% coverage on llm_client.py

tasks.name: Integration Test — Full Pipeline (No LLM)
tasks.description: Create tests/integration/test_pipeline_integration.py. Implement test_csv_clean_file: run full pipeline on clean_transactions.csv with minimal_config.yaml and llm=none; assert exit-equivalent success; assert ctx.integrity_report['overall_rating'] == 'CLEAN'; assert len(ctx.verified_findings) == 0; assert trail JSONL exists and verify() returns True; assert JSON report exists and is valid. Implement test_csv_with_anomalies: run on transactions_with_anomalies.csv with full_config.yaml; assert >= 15 findings; assert at least 1 high-severity finding; assert JSON report has all required keys; assert Markdown report contains findings table. Implement test_csv_with_nulls: assert IC-001 findings present. Implement test_csv_with_duplicates: assert IC-002 findings present. Implement test_excel_file: run on excel_single_sheet.xlsx; assert same results as equivalent CSV. Implement test_pipeline_resume: simulate checkpoint, clear layer 4+5 checkpoint, re-run with auto-resume, assert final output equivalent to fresh run.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 integration tests pass
test_csv_clean_file: verified_findings is empty list
test_csv_with_anomalies: >= 15 findings in verified_findings
Trail file exists and verify() returns True for all tests
No test makes real LLM API calls

tasks.name: Integration Test — Trail Immutability & Hash Chain
tasks.description: Create tests/integration/test_trail_integrity.py. Implement test_trail_hash_chain_valid: run pipeline, read trail JSONL, call TrailWriter.verify(), assert True. Implement test_trail_tampering_detected: run pipeline, read trail JSONL, modify the amount value in the 3rd entry (any field), write back, call verify(), assert False. Implement test_trail_append_only: run pipeline, check that JSONL line count matches expected (one per action type). Implement test_trail_entry_completeness: parse each trail entry, assert all required fields present (session_id, timestamp, agent_id, action_type, input_hash, output_hash, confidence). Implement test_session_id_consistency: all trail entries have same session_id. Implement test_file_permissions: assert trail file has mode 0o600 on Unix systems.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 trail tests pass
Tampering test detects single-character modification
All required trail entry fields present in every entry
Session ID consistent across all entries in a run

tasks.name: Integration Test — CLI Commands
tasks.description: Create tests/integration/test_cli.py using typer.testing.CliRunner. Implement test_run_help: assert exit code 0 and '--file' in output. Implement test_run_missing_file: assert exit code 1 and 'not found' in output. Implement test_run_success: run on clean_transactions.csv, assert exit code 0, assert output contains session ID and 'CLEAN'. Implement test_run_invalid_config: assert exit code 1. Implement test_validate_config_valid: assert '20 rules loaded' in output. Implement test_validate_config_invalid: assert exit code 1 and error details in output. Implement test_sessions_empty: assert 'No sessions found'. Implement test_sessions_after_run: run pipeline, then ave sessions --last 1, assert session ID in output. Implement test_check_llm_no_ollama: mock Ollama unavailable, assert 'not available' in output without exception.
acceptance_criteria: All 9 CLI tests pass using CliRunner (no subprocess)
test_run_success produces output files in temp directory
test_check_llm_no_ollama does not make real network calls

tasks.name: Performance Tests
tasks.description: Create tests/integration/test_performance.py. Add conftest.py fixture that generates large CSV files on demand: generate_csv(rows: int) uses Faker or random data with the transaction schema. Implement test_10k_rows_no_llm_under_60s: generate 10K row file, run pipeline without LLM, assert elapsed < 60 seconds. Implement test_50k_rows_no_llm_under_120s: generate 50K row file, run pipeline without LLM, assert elapsed < 120 seconds. Implement test_10k_rows_memory_under_500mb: use psutil to measure RSS before and after, assert delta < 500MB. Implement test_row_limit_enforcement: create 50001-row file, assert pipeline raises error with 'exceeds MVP limit' message. Mark tests with @pytest.mark.slow so they can be excluded with pytest -m 'not slow'.
acceptance_criteria: 10K rows test completes in < 60s on CI machine (2 CPU cores, 4GB RAM)
50K rows test completes in < 120s
Memory test uses psutil for measurement
Row limit test shows user-facing error message
edge_cases: Performance tests must use --no-llm to avoid network dependency
Time measurement must exclude fixture file generation time

tasks.name: End-to-End Test Scenarios
tasks.description: Create tests/integration/test_e2e.py with the 4 end-to-end scenarios from Section 9.4. Scenario 1 — Happy path: full pipeline on clean_transactions.csv with no LLM; verify exit code 0, CLEAN rating, empty findings, valid trail. Scenario 2 — Full anomaly detection: full pipeline on transactions_with_anomalies.csv; verify >= 15 findings, JSON schema validates, Markdown has required sections. Scenario 3 — Error recovery (checkpoint): run pipeline on large file, interrupt after layer 2 checkpoint saved (using monkeypatch to raise exception at start of layer 3), restart with force_resume=True, assert output equivalent to uninterrupted run. Scenario 4 — Rule validation: run validate_config on invalid_rules.yaml via CLI; assert non-zero exit and all 3 error types reported (duplicate ID, missing threshold, bad condition). Each scenario cleans up temp files in teardown.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 E2E scenarios pass
Scenario 3 checkpoint resume produces same finding count as fresh run
All test output written to temp dirs (no test pollution)
Tests run in < 3 minutes total (excluding performance-marked tests)

tasks.name: Large File Performance Optimization (Polars)
tasks.description: Create ave/utils/dataframe_adapter.py. Implement DataFrameAdapter class that wraps both Pandas and Polars DataFrames behind a unified interface. Methods: __init__(df: Any, backend: str = 'pandas'); to_pandas() -> pd.DataFrame; to_polars() -> pl.DataFrame; shape -> Tuple[int, int]; columns -> List[str]; dtypes -> dict; iter_rows(as_dict=True) -> Iterator; filter(mask: Any) -> 'DataFrameAdapter'; select_columns(cols: List[str]) -> 'DataFrameAdapter'; get_column(col: str) -> Any; apply_row_hash() -> List[str]; row_as_dict(idx: int) -> dict. In layer1_ingestion._switch_to_polars_if_large: wrap in DataFrameAdapter. Update rule_engine.py and integrity checks to use DataFrameAdapter.iter_rows() for row-by-row operations where vectorization is not possible. Use Polars expressions for vectorized operations when backend is 'polars'.
acceptance_criteria: DataFrameAdapter wraps Pandas and Polars with same method signatures
iter_rows() works correctly for both backends
Rule engine tests still pass after adapter integration
Polars backend used when row count >= threshold from config
edge_cases: Polars lazy evaluation: force evaluation before passing to adapter operations
Polars null type differs from Pandas NaN: normalize null handling in adapter

tasks.name: JSON Exporter Module
tasks.description: Create ave/export/json_exporter.py. Implement JsonExporter class with generate(report: dict, output_path: Path) -> Path. The generate method must: validate report dict has all required top-level keys from Section 2.5.2; convert any non-JSON-serializable types (datetime, Path, Decimal objects) using a custom JSONEncoder that converts: datetime -> ISO string, Path -> str, Decimal -> float, set -> list, dataclass -> dict via dataclasses.asdict(); write with json.dump(report, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, cls=AveJSONEncoder); return output_path. Ensure ensure_ascii=False to preserve Vietnamese characters in output.
acceptance_criteria: Output JSON is valid (json.loads succeeds)
Vietnamese characters preserved in JSON (not escaped as \uXXXX)
datetime objects in report serialized as ISO 8601 strings
Output file has .json extension and indent=2 formatting
edge_cases: Report dict with nested dataclass objects: ensure recursive serialization
NaN float values (from Pandas): serialize as null in JSON (not 'NaN' string)

tasks.name: Config Fixture Files — Complete
tasks.description: Complete tests/fixtures/minimal_config.yaml and tests/fixtures/full_config.yaml as defined in T034. minimal_config.yaml: only pipeline.max_rows and rules_file (pointing to rules/default_rules.yaml), no column definitions, llm.enabled: false. full_config.yaml: all fields from Section 4.4 including: pipeline settings, ingestion with skip_rows, columns section defining amount (currency, VND, min_value: 0, required: true), transaction_date (date, min_date: 2020-01-01), vendor_id (text, pattern: ^VN-\d{4}$), integrity settings (null_threshold_default: 0.0, outlier_std_multiplier: 3.0), llm section (enabled: false, provider: ollama, model: mistral:7b, batch_size: 50), approval_thresholds (level_1: 100000000, level_2: 500000000), fiscal_year_end_month: 12, output (formats: [json, markdown], pdf_enabled: false). invalid_rules.yaml: 3 rules — one with duplicate ID 'R001', one with condition 'gt' and no threshold, one with condition 'banana' (invalid type).
acceptance_criteria: load_config('minimal_config.yaml') succeeds with Pydantic validation
load_config('full_config.yaml') succeeds and all fields populated
load_config with invalid_rules.yaml passed as rules_file fails with 3 specific errors
YAML files have comments explaining each non-obvious field

tasks.name: Error Message Quality Pass
tasks.description: Review and improve all user-facing error messages across the codebase. For every AveError subclass raised, ensure the message follows the pattern: WHAT went wrong | WHY it happened | HOW to fix it. Implement an ErrorMessageCatalog in ave/exceptions.py as a dict of error codes to message templates: E001 FILE_NOT_FOUND: 'File not found: {path}. Verify the path exists and you have read permissions.'; E002 FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED: 'Unsupported file format: {ext}. AVE supports CSV (.csv) and Excel (.xlsx) files.'; E003 ENCODING_FAILED: 'Could not detect file encoding. Try specifying --encoding utf-8 or --encoding latin-1.'; E004 CONFIG_NOT_FOUND: 'Config file not found: {path}. Create one using: ave validate-config --template'; E005 ROW_LIMIT_EXCEEDED: 'File has {count} rows, exceeding the 50,000-row MVP limit. Use --max-rows to raise the limit or process a subset.'; E006 RULE_INVALID: 'Rule {rule_id}: {field} is invalid. {reason}'; E007 LLM_UNAVAILABLE: 'LLM provider {provider} is not available. Run: ave check-llm --provider {provider}'; E008 OLLAMA_MODEL_MISSING: 'Ollama model {model} not found. Install it with: ollama pull {model}'. Update all raise sites to use catalog messages.
outputs: Updated error messages in all modules
acceptance_criteria: Every AveError message answers: what, why, how-to-fix
Error code E001-E008 all present in catalog
CLI shows error messages without Python tracebacks (tracebacks only with --verbose)
LLM unavailable error includes specific fix command

tasks.name: Installation & README Documentation
tasks.description: Create README.md with complete installation and usage documentation. Sections: ## Installation (pip install ave; pip install ave[pdf] for PDF support); ## Quick Start (5-step guide: install Ollama, pull model, prepare CSV, create config, run ave run); ## Configuration (YAML schema reference linking to Section 4.4); ## Rules DSL (condition types table with examples); ## CLI Reference (all commands with all options); ## Output Files (description of JSON/Markdown/PDF/JSONL outputs); ## Performance (row limits, benchmarks); ## Troubleshooting (top 5 issues with solutions). Also create docs/AUDIT_TRAIL_FORMAT.md explaining the JSONL format and hash chain verification. Create docs/RULES_GUIDE.md explaining how to write custom rules with 5 worked examples.
acceptance_criteria: Quick Start guide can be followed by new user in < 30 minutes
All CLI options documented with types and defaults
All 15 condition types documented with YAML examples
Troubleshooting covers: Ollama not running, encoding issues, large files, missing WeasyPrint
README.md renders correctly as GitHub Markdown

tasks.name: DataFrameAdapter Integration into Layer 2
tasks.description: Update ave/pipeline/layer2_integrity.py to use DataFrameAdapter. Replace direct Pandas/Polars calls with adapter methods. Specifically: IC-002 row hashing must use adapter.apply_row_hash() which handles both backends; IC-010 outlier detection must use adapter.get_column() and handle both numpy arrays and Polars Series. Ensure all Finding creation uses adapter.row_as_dict(idx) to capture row data. Run existing integrity tests to verify no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: All existing integrity unit tests still pass
IC-002 works correctly with Polars DataFrames
No direct pandas imports in layer2_integrity.py (use adapter only)

tasks.name: DataFrameAdapter Integration into Layer 3
tasks.description: Update ave/pipeline/layer3_anomaly.py and ave/engines/rule_engine.py to use DataFrameAdapter. Rule engine evaluate() must accept DataFrameAdapter. Vectorized evaluators (gt, lt, eq, etc.) must use adapter.get_column() and handle Polars Series vs Pandas Series. Row-level evaluators (weekend_transaction, compound) must use adapter.iter_rows(). Update _prepare_llm_batches to use adapter.iter_rows(). Ensure R018 frequency check works with both backends. Run existing anomaly tests to verify no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: All rule engine unit tests still pass
Rule evaluation works with both Pandas and Polars DataFrames
LLM batch preparation works with Polars adapter

tasks.name: Full Regression Test Run & Coverage Report
tasks.description: Configure pytest with coverage in pyproject.toml: [tool.pytest.ini_options] with testpaths=['tests'], addopts='--cov=ave --cov-report=term-missing --cov-report=html'. Set minimum coverage thresholds: fail if total coverage < 80%. Create Makefile or justfile with targets: test (run all tests), test-unit (run unit only), test-integration (run integration), test-slow (include performance tests), coverage (generate HTML report), lint (ruff check + ruff format --check). Run full test suite. Fix any failures. Ensure all unit tests pass, all integration tests pass, performance tests run within limits. Generate coverage report. Document any gaps > 10% uncovered lines in key modules.
acceptance_criteria: pytest exits with code 0 (all tests pass)
Total coverage >= 80%
ave/engines/rule_engine.py coverage >= 90%
ave/engines/trail_writer.py coverage >= 95%
ave/utils/normalization.py coverage >= 90%
No test takes > 30 seconds individually (excluding @pytest.mark.slow tests)

tasks.name: Ruff Lint & Format Pass
tasks.description: Run ruff check . and ruff format . on entire codebase. Fix all lint errors. Configure ruff.toml: line-length=100, target-version='py311', select=['E','W','F','I','N','UP','B','C4','SIM','PIE'], ignore=['E501' for long strings in YAML templates]. Ensure all imports are sorted (isort-compatible via ruff). Ensure no unused imports. Ensure all type annotations use Python 3.10+ union syntax (X | Y instead of Union[X, Y]). Add pre-commit config (.pre-commit-config.yaml) with ruff hooks for future enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: ruff check . exits with 0 errors
ruff format --check . exits with 0 errors
All imports use Python 3.10+ union syntax
.pre-commit-config.yaml installs and runs successfully

tasks.name: Final Integration Smoke Test & Release Prep
tasks.description: Perform final end-to-end smoke test on a clean Python 3.11 virtualenv. Steps: (1) Create fresh venv; (2) pip install -e .; (3) Run ave --help; (4) Run ave validate-config --config rules/default_rules.yaml; (5) Run ave run --file tests/fixtures/transactions_with_anomalies.csv --config tests/fixtures/full_config.yaml --no-llm --output-dir /tmp/ave_smoke_test; (6) Verify output files exist: _report.json, _report.md, _trail.jsonl; (7) Run ave sessions --last 1; (8) Verify JSON report parses correctly and has >= 10 findings; (9) Run ave export --session <id> --format markdown; (10) Run TrailWriter.verify() on trail file. Document any issues found. Update CHANGELOG.md with MVP feature list. Tag version 0.1.0 in pyproject.toml.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 smoke test steps complete without errors
JSON report has >= 10 findings from anomalies fixture
Trail file verify() returns True
CHANGELOG.md documents all MVP features
ave --help output matches README CLI reference section
